Definition.text: More is a multiplicative modifier token used to scale effects, including damage, by multiplication rather than by arithmetic addition.

Purpose.text: Apply multiplicative scaling so modifier effects compose with additive sources in a separate bucket.

Core Rules.text: Increased/Reduced terms are additive, while More/Less terms multiply with each other and with the additive result.

Internal Rules.text: Each qualifying More entry is represented as a dedicated factor and applied to the already resolved base+additive total.

Calculation Order.text: 1) Base value calculation 2) additive (Increased/Reduced) resolution 3) More/Less factor application 4) post-rule caps and rounding/precision constraints.

Priority Rules.text: Mechanic-specific text overrides the generic More bucket when scope is narrowed to a component, phase, or target.

Preconditions.text: A stat or sub-stat must be marked to accept More/Less scaling and a valid source mechanic must attach the modifier.

Postconditions.text: Affected quantity is multiplied by all More/Less factors in scope rather than merged into an additive pool.

Limitations.text: More cannot be treated as Increased with sign inversion; only systems that explicitly define linear behavior can do so.

Caps.text: No universal More token cap; final values remain bounded by recipient stat systems.

Formula.text: final = base_after_additive * Π(1 + each_more_i) * Π(1 - each_less_j)

Conflicts.text: Confusing More with Increased leads to incorrect total scaling; explicit mechanic scope always overrides generic combination assumptions.

Exceptions.text: Some skills or support-given effects gate More to a subcomponent and do not apply globally.

Hidden Rules.text: Model More and Less as separate bucket objects in storage to preserve future patch-level behavior changes.

Developer Notes.text: Do not normalize More into additive percent; preserve multiplicative order and factor boundaries.

Historical Changes.text: No explicit mechanic definition reset was captured in this pass; behavior remains tracked as additive + multiplicative bucket model.

text.note: Token remains a multiplicative modifier family; no official source in this pass indicates core semantics reversal.

text: Two instances 20% More and 30% More produce 1.2 * 1.3 = 1.56 multiplier.
More modifiers do not apply as +50%; they scale by factor.
More can be merged linearly with other More values
